text: Built an image compressor that runs 100% in your browser. No uploads, no servers, no tracking. WebAssembly + Web Workers do all the work locally. Try it: https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
Your vacation photos should NOT travel to some random server to be shrunk. Pixshrink compresses in-browser. Your files never leave your device. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
ブラウザ内で完結する画像圧縮ツールを作りました。アップロード不要、サーバー不要。WebAssemblyでローカル処理。https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
tinypng vs squoosh vs pixshrink: only pixshrink is fully client-side, ad-free, open-source, and zero-account. Same quality, way less creepy. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org

Compresor de imágenes 100% en el navegador. Sin servidores, sin subidas, sin tracking. WebAssembly hace el trabajo local. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
Squoosh is great. But Google owns it. If that bothers you, Pixshrink is the indie, no-telemetry alternative. Drop a 50MB JPG, get a 5MB JPG, never leave your browser. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org

TIL: a 4K photo can lose 80% of its size just by re-encoding at quality 85 with mozjpeg. Pixshrink does exactly that, in your browser, in under a second. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
Bildkomprimierung ohne Konto, ohne Werbung, ohne Upload. Pixshrink läuft komplett im Browser. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org

How does Pixshrink hit 80% compression with no quality loss? Mozjpeg at q85 + webp fallback + smart downscaling. All in your browser. All open source. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
compressors that upload your photos to the cloud in 2026 is the same energy as restaurants that don't have a bathroom. just why. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org

Speed test: compressed 47 photos (avg 3.2MB each) in 18 seconds with Pixshrink. Zero network requests after page load. No data sent. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org

Did you know most 'compressors' actually re-upload your files to a CDN? Pixshrink processes everything with Web Workers. Your CPU does the work, not someone else's server. https://pixshrink.gamechill.org
